api.doc: Recursively snapshot path -> "size:sha-like(content)" for every file.
api.name: snapshot

api.doc: Tiny content hash (FNV-1a): avoids a crypto import and is deterministic.
api.name: hash

api.doc: Normalize a relative import against the importing file's directory, resolving "." and ".." segments. Returns a posix path with no leading "./".
api.name: normalizeRelative

api.doc: Resolve a relative import to a repo file, trying common extensions and index files. External and bare imports return null and become no edge.
api.name: resolveImport

api.doc: Build an adjacency map { relPath -> [relPath, ...] } from per-file imports. Only edges that resolve to another repo file are kept.
api.name: buildImportGraph

api.doc: Degree centrality: out-edges of a node plus in-edges pointing at it.
api.name: centrality

api.doc: PageRank over the import graph. Fixed iteration count keeps it deterministic. Dangling nodes (no out-edges) redistribute their rank uniformly.
api.name: pagerank

api.doc: Rank files by a normalized composite of churn, centrality, and PageRank. Returns a sorted list of { path, churn, centrality, pagerank, score }, highest first.
api.name: attentionRank
api.signature: export function attentionRank(paths, { churn = new Map(), centralityMap = new Map(), pagerankMap = new Map() } = {})

api.doc: Report whether the import graph has a cycle and one example cycle, reusing the shared cycle detector so the snapshot can warn about circular dependencies.
api.name: findCycle

api.doc: Resolve an alias to an active, in-window key entry in the allowlist.
api.name: resolveActiveKey
api.signature: export function resolveActiveKey(peerKeys, alias, now = new Date())

api.doc: Full ordered verification. options.skipContentGate runs only the signature checks (steps 3 to 5), used when the caller already ran the schema and redaction gate.
api.name: verifyPacket
api.signature: export function verifyPacket(packet, peerKeys, { now = new Date(), skipContentGate = false } = {})

api.doc: Raw 32-byte public key as base64, accepting either a public or private KeyObject.
api.name: publicKeyB64

api.doc: Public KeyObject from a raw 32-byte base64 public key.
api.name: publicKeyFromB64

api.doc: Private KeyObject from base64 PKCS8 DER (the env-secret format).
api.name: privateKeyFromB64Pkcs8

api.doc: Short fingerprint for out-of-band key comparison (ADR-017 enrollment): the first 16 hex characters of sha256 over the raw public key bytes.
api.name: fingerprint

api.doc: Parse a raw value string from after the colon, handling inline comments and quoted strings. Returns the trimmed scalar text or empty string.
api.name: extractRawValue

api.doc: Count leading spaces to determine nesting depth.
api.name: indentOf

api.doc: Parse an array of non-empty, non-comment lines into a nested object. Each entry is { indent, key, rawValue }.
api.name: parseEntries

api.doc: A config fixture with distinct maker/checker models and a models registry.
api.name: cfg

api.doc: A short, stable id from a string. Hex keeps it deterministic across platforms.
api.name: short

api.doc: Build the path dictionary from walked files. Returns { paths, pathIdByPath } where `paths` is the serializable { id -> relPath } map and `pathIdByPath` is the reverse lookup callers use to reference p
api.name: buildPathDictionary

api.doc: Build the symbol dictionary from API entries. Each entry carries its anchor, owning path id, name, and line, so an anchor resolves to an exact location.
api.name: buildSymbolDictionary

api.doc: Domain separation tag binds a signature to this packet type and version so a signature over one structure cannot be replayed as another.
api.name: PACKET_DOMAIN

api.doc: The exact bytes a packet signature covers: the domain tag followed by the JCS of the packet with its signature object removed.
api.name: signedBytes

api.doc: Resolve the adapter for a path by extension, defaulting to the generic fallback.
api.name: getAdapter

api.doc: Extract from one file, guarding against any adapter error so a single bad file never aborts a whole snapshot.
api.name: extractFile

api.doc: Hash raw file bytes (Buffer or string) into a prefixed digest.
api.name: hashFileContent

api.doc: Hash an arbitrary string, used for oversized or unreadable files where content is represented by a stable stand-in rather than its bytes.
api.name: hashString

api.doc: Build a Merkle tree from file leaves. `entries` is [{ relPath, hash }]. Returns { root, nodes } where nodes maps every directory path (root is ".") to its hash.
api.name: buildMerkleTree

api.doc: File-level diff between two { relPath -> hash } maps. Returns sorted lists of added, removed, and changed paths. Directory node hashes (from buildMerkleTree) let a caller skip re-extracting an unchang
api.name: diffMerkle

api.doc: Observe concrete facts about a repository. Pure with respect to its inputs: it only reads the filesystem under root and returns a plain object.
api.name: detectRepoFacts

api.doc: A criterion entry: a stable id, the framework and level, whether the observed facts satisfy it, and the evidence or remediation note.
api.name: criterion
api.signature: function criterion(id, framework, level, met, evidence)

api.doc: Map observed facts to criteria across the supported frameworks. Pure.
api.name: mapToCriteria

api.doc: Resolve runner and model settings for a named role. * * @param {object} cfg - Parsed config object (output of parseFlatYaml or loadConfig). * @param {string} role - One of "maker", "checker", "self-go
api.name: resolveRole
validate
api.signature: export function validate(schema, value, path = "$", errors = [])

api.doc: Helper reused by the mapping test.
api.name: makeRepoOnce

api.doc: 4. The two protected-path surfaces must agree. CODEOWNERS is what GitHub enforces; protected_paths_extra is what the engine reads. If they disagree, a path is protected in name only (the bin/ gap that
api.name: dirsFromCodeowners

api.doc: Build a minimal passing temp repo and return the path. Caller must rmSync(tmp, {recursive:true}).
api.name: makeMinimalRepo

api.doc: Mask every matching secret in `text`. Returns { text, redactions } where each redaction records the pattern name and how many matches it masked.
api.name: redactText

api.doc: Extract the first fenced json block that appears after the "## Promoted" heading.
api.name: readPromotedLearnings

api.doc: Helper
api.name: execSync

api.doc: Returns an array of { name } objects for every pattern that matches text.
api.name: scanForSecrets

api.doc: True when the first path segment of a branch name equals a denylisted token. * Matching is case-insensitive. "feature/ai-adapter" is allowed because the * first segment is "feature"; only a leading "a
api.name: isModelIdentifierBranch

api.doc: Resolve the branch under review from CI environment variables. Prefers the * pull request head ref, then the push ref name. Returns an empty string when * neither is set so callers can fall back to a
api.name: resolveBranchName

api.doc: Minimal, dependency-free scan for top-level YAML job names under `jobs:`.
api.name: parseCiJobNames

api.doc: Extremely small YAML-ish key:value reader for flat config files. Good enough to inspect schema_version and the boolean arming levers without a YAML dep.
api.name: parseFlatYaml

api.doc: (a) Schema collision: target has .modonome/ with incompatible config.
api.name: checkSchemaCollision

api.doc: (b) CI job name conflict: target's CI files use Modonome job names.
api.name: checkCiJobConflict

api.doc: (c) Script shadowing: target has scripts/ that shadow Modonome scripts.
api.name: checkScriptShadowing

api.doc: (d) Env var pollution: MODONOME_* env vars set that override safe defaults. Reads from the current process environment (the shell preparing to embed) AND statically inspects, read-only, the target's `
api.name: checkEnvPollution
api.signature: export async function checkEnvPollution(targetDir, env = process.env)

api.doc: (e) Dependency conflict: target has deps that conflict with Modonome requirements.
api.name: checkDependencyConflict

api.doc: (f) Prompt injection risk: governance-override patterns in the target. Trusted locations (.modonome/, schemas/, CI dirs) are scanned exhaustively; for the rest of the repo we scan source-bearing files
api.name: checkPromptInjection

api.doc: (g) Node version incompatibility: target requires Node < 18.
api.name: checkNodeVersion

api.doc: Dependency-free token accounting for snapshot tiers. The estimate is a heuristic (about four characters per token) that needs no tokenizer and no network, which keeps the utility portable. It is used
api.name: estimateTokens

api.doc: Greedily keep pre-ranked items until the token budget is spent. `sizeFn` returns the token cost of an item. A falsy or non-finite budget keeps everything. Returns { kept, dropped, tokens } so the call
api.name: budgetTier

api.doc: Pure: attach a signature object to a packet using the given private key.
api.name: signPacket
api.signature: export function signPacket(packet, privateKeyObject, { keyAlias, signedAt })

api.doc: Parse RELEASE-EVIDENCE.md to extract gate counts and autonomy status
api.name: parseEvidence

api.doc: Count work items by state
api.name: countWorkItems

api.doc: Parse version from .modonome/version
api.name: readVersion

api.doc: Update site/index.html with live data
api.name: updateSite

api.doc: Verify site data matches evidence (used in CI gate)
api.name: verifySiteData

api.doc: Build the adjacency map { state: [to, ...] } from the transition list. When includeCapGuard is false, cap_guard edges are dropped: those are the sanctioned bounded-retry escapes and must not count as
api.name: buildAdjacency

api.doc: reaches(adjacency, start, targets) -> bool Whether any node in `targets` is reachable from `start` along the edges.
api.name: reaches

api.doc: Chaos test helper: any call must either return errors cleanly OR not throw. A crash or hang is a failure.
api.name: noThrow

api.doc: Wrap guard-ratchet call with a hard 5-second timeout.
api.name: ratchetWithTimeout

api.doc: Safety rules beyond structural validation. These keep a config from claiming an armed posture without the controls that make arming safe. Note on arming levers: config values such as autonomy_enabled
api.name: safetyErrors

api.doc: Sample-app captures: real maker and checker runs recorded under examples/<app>/runs/. These directories are committed (unlike the gitignored .modonome/runs/), so summarizing them stays reproducible fr
api.name: listCaptures
Card
api.signature: export type Card = { number: string; expired: boolean };

api.name: refund
api.signature: export function refund(card: Card, amount: number): RefundResult

api.doc: Safe defaults for every lever. Migration fills any missing key from here.
api.name: SAFE_DEFAULTS

api.doc: Recompute file hashes and the Merkle root directly from disk. Used by --verify.
api.name: recomputeMerkle

api.doc: Send requests to a fresh server process and resolve once every expected id has replied. The child is killed as soon as the responses arrive, which avoids the stdin-close race in batch mode.
api.name: rpc

api.doc: Resolve the list of files to scan. If a path argument is supplied use it directly; otherwise walk examples/runs/metrics.jsonl via readdirSync.
api.name: resolveFiles

api.doc: Build the small file helpers a detector needs, bound to one target directory.
api.name: helpers

api.doc: Detect the primary stack. Returns { name, pm, gates } exactly as the dry-run sweep expects, plus { entrypoints, commands } for the snapshot signature.
api.name: detectStack

api.doc: Paths that must never be auto-merged. Same list the dry-run sweep reports.
api.name: detectProtected

api.doc: Repo instruction files an agent should read first.
api.name: detectInstructions

api.doc: Rank files by how often they changed in recent git history. The dry-run sweep uses the default limit of 3; the snapshot passes a larger limit to score churn across the whole tree. Returns [] when git
api.name: detectHotFiles

api.doc: Build a synthetic 1000-line diff that is clean (no gaming patterns).
api.name: buildLargeDiff

api.doc: Read all .mjs files in a directory (non-recursive by default).
api.name: listMjs

api.doc: Extract import specifiers from a file's source text. Only matches actual import statements (not comments or JSDoc).
api.name: extractImportSpecifiers

api.doc: Base valid packet factory: returns a fresh object each call.
api.name: makePacket

api.doc: Check that a section appears as a Markdown heading (h1-h6), so a one-line ADR with the section words buried in prose cannot game the gate.
api.name: hasHeading

api.doc: Compile one gitignore-style pattern into a tester over a posix relative path. Supported: comments, negation (!), leading / (anchored), trailing / (directory), * (within a segment), ** (across segments
api.name: compilePattern

api.doc: Build an ignore predicate for a repo root. The predicate takes a posix relative path and returns true when the path should be excluded. Later patterns win, so a negation can re-include a path a broad
api.name: loadIgnore

api.doc: Walk a repository into a sorted list of files. Symlinks are skipped to avoid cycles and escapes. Returns [{ relPath, absPath, size }] ordered by relPath.
api.name: walkRepo
api.signature: export function walkRepo(root, { ignore = () => false, maxDepth = 12 } = {})

api.doc: Run preflight in --json mode against a fixture. Returns { code, report, raw }. A clean environment is used so the host's own MODONOME_* shell does not leak into the env-pollution check.
api.name: runPreflight

api.doc: A lease is "live" if it has an owner and an unexpired lease_expires_at. The lease holder is recorded as lease_owner (the field this swap writes) or, for older items, the schema's `owner` field; either
api.name: leaseHolder

api.doc: tryTransition(item, fromState, toState, writerId, now) -> result { ok: true, item } swap succeeded; item is a fresh copy { ok: false, conflict: "<reason>" } swap refused; item is left untouched `now`
api.name: tryTransition
api.signature: export function tryTransition(item, fromState, toState, writerId, now = new Date())

api.doc: Detect binary content by scanning a prefix for a null byte.
api.name: isBinary

api.doc: Build the full snapshot for a repository root.
api.name: buildSnapshot

api.doc: Resolve adjacency into a sorted edge list of dictionary path ids.
api.name: buildEdgeList
renderMarkdown
api.signature: function renderMarkdown({ generatedFor, merkleRoot, files, totalBytes, map })

api.doc: Read a light governance posture from the target config and environment. It never arms anything; it only reports posture so a snapshot can double as a status probe.
api.name: readGovernance

api.doc: Resolve and validate a full cycle plan without calling any model. Pure: it reads the passed config and runId and throws on any policy violation. This is the testable core of the harness; the execute p
api.name: planCycle

api.doc: Build the child-process environment for a role invocation. When the resolved model carries a base_url (a local, self-hosted, or gateway endpoint), route the CLI there by setting ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, wh
api.name: buildRunnerEnv

api.doc: Execute a plan. Refuses a hosted run when the budget is zero. Runs the maker, then the checker, each as a distinct CLI invocation with its own model and identity.
api.name: runCycle
api.signature: export function runCycle(opts, { execute, cfg, runId, env = process.env })

api.doc: True when a name or email belongs to a denylisted agent or vendor identity. * Real automation such as dependabot is allowed; only coding-agent and model * vendor identities are rejected.
api.name: isForbiddenIdentity

api.doc: Parse `git log` output where each commit is one line of * "authorName<TAB>authorEmail<TAB>committerName<TAB>committerEmail<TAB>shortSha". * Returns the commits whose author or committer is a forbidden
api.name: findForbiddenCommits

api.doc: parseArgs(argv) -> { diff, "work-item" } map of fixture paths by gate arg name.
api.name: parseArgs

api.doc: gateOrder(graph) -> [...] the gates in dependency-first topological order. topoSort orders a gate ahead of the gates it points to, so reverse to put each gate's dependencies before the gate itself.
api.name: gateOrder

api.name: runPipeline

api.name: isCyclic

api.name: topoSort

api.doc: Collect every node mentioned either as a key or as a neighbour value.
api.name: collectNodes

api.doc: The authoritative arming gate. A config file the agent can write can never arm the engine on its own: arming requires the MODONOME_ARMED=true environment variable, which lives in CI or operator scope,
api.name: resolveArming

api.doc: Schema-conformant event line using "event" field (not "type").
api.name: makeEvent

api.doc: gateGraphErrors(graph) -> { errors: [...], order: [...] } `errors` lists every defect (dangling edge or cycle); when it is empty, `order` holds a topological ordering with dependencies before dependen
api.name: gateGraphErrors

api.name: adrNumbers

api.doc: Front-matter parsing for canonical uniqueness and advisory presence.
api.name: parseFrontMatter

api.doc: Substitute every ${VAR} from env. Throw if a referenced variable is unset, so a missing identity or branch fails loudly instead of rendering an empty value into a model prompt.
api.name: renderPrompt

api.doc: Run validate-config.mjs against a given config path.
api.name: runValidateConfig

api.doc: Run guard-ratchet.mjs with a --diff fixture.

CartService: manages user shopping carts stored in memory.
CheckoutService: drives the checkout flow from cart to order.
InventoryService: tracks stock levels for products in memory.
NotificationService: records notifications sent to users.
PaymentProcessor: wraps a payment gateway with amount conversion.
Composition root for the demo app.
Negative control: intentional syntax error in example app
